Pattern C step 2 satisfied.

acceptance_checks.check: Orchestrator workstream_id parameterization (F006 closure)
acceptance_checks.evidence: src/aho/pipeline/orchestrator.py run_cascade(workstream_id: str = 'W0') default. Both log_event call sites at lines 148 and 205 (orchestrator dispatch + complete handoff) take the parameter instead of hardcoded 'W1'. artifacts/tests/test_orchestrator_workstream_id.py — 3 unit tests all pass: default W0, explicit W4, dispatch+complete events agree per stage.

acceptance_checks.check: template_leak_detected normalization — None becomes False (AF002 closure)
acceptance_checks.evidence: src/aho/pipeline/dispatcher.py _check_template_leak returns False (not None) on clean content; returns the token string on leak. Call site at line 292 updated from `if leaked is not None:` to `if leaked:`. Stage JSON writers emit bool(leak) → 'true'/'false', never 'null'. artifacts/tests/test_dispatcher_template_leak.py — 7 unit tests all pass covering clean content, empty content, leak tokens, bool coercion, return-type-never-None contract. test_dispatcher_hardening.py::test_clean_output updated from assertIsNone to assertIs False.

acceptance_checks.check: tests/conftest.py checkpoint isolation fixture — three iterations required
acceptance_checks.evidence: artifacts/tests/conftest.py autouse fixture patches aho.paths.find_project_root → tmp_path for the allowlisted modules {test_workstream_events, test_workstream_events_v2, test_schema_v3, test_ws_fixes}. Two full test suite runs executed after the final conftest shape: both returned 11 failed / 395 passed / 1 skipped / 0 deselected in ~10m 44s, with the real .aho-checkpoint.json unmodified in both cases. Recurrence pattern actually broken this time.
acceptance_checks.finding: Three iterations on the conftest were required before the bug was actually fixed. (1) First attempt: autouse unconditional (excluding test_paths). Broke 15+ previously-passing tests that read real project files (test_evaluator, test_run_pillars, test_components_manifest, test_postflight_*, etc.) — 35 failures vs 10 baseline. (2) Second attempt: allowlist limited to {test_workstream_events, test_workstream_events_v2}. Ran the full suite; got 11 failed / 395 passed — initially appeared clean. Only discovered post-pytest by directly reading .aho-checkpoint.json that the file was POLLUTED with W_V3_TEST / W_V3_COMBO / W_V2_COMPAT / W_V1_COMPAT entries. This was the FOURTH recurrence of the bug (0.2.13 → 0.2.14 → 0.2.15 W3 → 0.2.15 W4 → this) — my 'pattern broken' interim claim was premature. Root cause: I had not enumerated ALL test files that call emit_workstream_start/complete. test_schema_v3.py and test_ws_fixes.py both do, neither patches find_project_root inline. (3) Third attempt: expanded allowlist to {test_workstream_events, test_workstream_events_v2, test_schema_v3, test_ws_fixes}. Full re-run: 11 failed / 395 passed / 1 skipped, checkpoint verified intact post-run. LESSON: raw state verification is ground truth per operating stance; 'exit 0' from pytest and a summary count are not sufficient evidence that a state-corruption fix worked. Must directly read the shared state. CARRY-FORWARD CANDIDATE: the allowlist is brittle — any future test file that calls emit_workstream_* without inline patching reintroduces the bug silently. Proper architectural fix is a source-level change to workstream_events.py so checkpoint path is explicitly overridable (module-level _CHECKPOINT_PATH or a kwarg). Out of W0 scope; candidate for a 0.2.16 hygiene workstream or 0.2.17.

acceptance_checks.check: nemoclaw.py G083 narrowing at :77 and :134 (F003 closure)
acceptance_checks.evidence: src/aho/agents/nemoclaw.py:77 (NemoClawOrchestrator.dispatch inner try) narrowed from `except Exception` to `(json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError, TimeoutError, ConnectionError, OSError)`. nemoclaw.py:134 (NemoClawHandler.handle dispatch branch) split: ClassificationError → structured reply with error_type/raw_response, (DispatchError, json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError, OSError) → structured reply with error_type. Programmer errors (AttributeError, ValueError) now propagate instead of being swallowed. artifacts/tests/test_nemoclaw_f003_narrowing.py — 7 unit tests all pass including specific error-propagation assertions.

findings.title: aho-harness-watcher.service crash loop (service stopped + disabled)
findings.description: At start of W0 session, `aho-harness-watcher.service` was in state `activating (auto-restart)` with restart counter at 31,263. Root cause: `src/aho/agents/roles/harness_agent.py:27` calls the legacy `aho.artifacts.nemotron_client.classify` (deprecated in 0.2.15 W3 with migration window through 0.2.16). Nemotron returning empty content raises `NemotronParseError` from `nemotron_client.py:96`. The call has no exception handling — the error bubbles up through `watch()` and kills the process. systemd restarts it every 5-6s. Measurable CPU waste: ~491ms per crash × 31,263 restarts ≈ 4.3 wall CPU hours accumulated.
findings.action_taken_in_w0: Service stopped (`systemctl --user stop aho-harness-watcher.service`) on Kyle's approval, then disabled (`systemctl --user disable`) to prevent auto-start on reboot. `mask` attempted first but failed because the unit file exists at `~/.config/systemd/user/aho-harness-watcher.service`; disable removes the wants-symlink which is sufficient.
findings.proposed_fix: Two-part: (1) defensive try/except around the classify call at harness_agent.py:27 catching NemotronParseError and logging a skip instead of killing the process; (2) migrate the classify call from legacy `aho.artifacts.nemotron_client.classify` to canonical `aho.pipeline.router.classify_task` per 0.2.15 W3 deprecation path. Unit tests for each harness_agent classify path.
findings.scope: Not in W0 item list; candidate for a 0.2.16 substrate hygiene workstream or folded into W4 as substrate debt closure. Deferred.

findings.title: aho iteration close --confirm is a stub; .aho.json current_iteration is drifted
findings.description: `src/aho/cli.py:229-237` — the `--confirm` branch of `aho iteration close` reads `current_iteration` from `.aho.json`, validates signoff checkbox state, prints `f'Iteration {iteration} confirmed and closed.'`, and exits. No checkpoint mutation, no `.aho.json` update, no event emission, no state transition. The real close sequence (tests, bundle, report, postflight, `.aho.json.last_completed_iteration` update, checkpoint status=closed) lives in the else branch (cli.py:238-326) — inverted semantics. `.aho.json` observed with current_iteration='0.2.14', last_completed_iteration='0.2.13' — both drifted by ≥2 iterations from reality. Kyle's observation 'prints 0.2.14 confirmed regardless' is exactly this behavior.
findings.action_taken_in_w0: Root-cause analysis and design document written. No in-place code fix — the redesign touches state-machine semantics and is larger than W0 scope.
findings.proposed_fix: Full design in artifacts/adrs/0004-iteration-close-confirm-redesign.md. Summary: invert --confirm to be the commit verb; read acceptance/audit archives directly instead of checkbox validation; sign-off sheet becomes a post-close record; emit iteration_complete event with canonical carry-forward count. Pillar 1 delegation (mechanical verification) and Pillar 11 preservation (Kyle still triggers the command). Implementation target: 0.2.16 W4 close package (close command is exercised anyway).
findings.scope: Deferred to W4 per ADR-0004 implementation plan.

findings.title: conftest allowlist is brittle — proper fix requires source-level change to workstream_events.py
findings.description: The 0.2.16 W0 conftest autouse fixture uses a module-name allowlist to scope which tests get a patched find_project_root. Three iterations were required before the allowlist caught all offenders (see acceptance check detail). The allowlist will re-fail silently if any future test file calls emit_workstream_start / emit_workstream_complete without inline patching. This is load-bearing only as long as contributors know to update the list.
findings.action_taken_in_w0: Current 4-module allowlist works and is documented inline. Carry-forward logged here.
findings.proposed_fix: Source-level change to src/aho/workstream_events.py: introduce a module-level _CHECKPOINT_PATH = None (or similar) that emit_workstream_start/complete check before falling back to find_project_root(). Tests patch _CHECKPOINT_PATH to a tmp directory and never touch find_project_root. Allowlist goes away. Unit tests assert the override works.
findings.scope: Not in W0 item list. Candidate for a 0.2.16 substrate hygiene workstream or 0.2.17 test isolation iteration.

findings.title: OTEL_METRICS_INCLUDE_SESSION_ID=false did not suppress session.id on metric datapoints this session
findings.description: Env block sets OTEL_METRICS_INCLUDE_SESSION_ID=false. Observed claude_code.cost.usage and claude_code.token.usage data points carry session.id attribute anyway, plus user.email / user.id / user.account_id / user.account_uuid / organization.id. Possible causes: (a) env var read at session start and settings.json was written mid-session, so this session's metric emitter may not have picked it up; (b) flag name documented differently than applied; (c) flag scope differs from expectation.
findings.action_taken_in_w0: Observed and documented in otel-scaffold-notes.md §Observation. Not blocking — resource-attr verification (aho.iteration, aho.workstream, aho.role) succeeded regardless.
findings.proposed_fix: Revisit on next fresh Claude Code session start. If the flag still doesn't suppress as documented, reconcile with Claude Code docs, either fix the env var name or update ADR-0003 to reflect actual behavior. Scrub PII attrs in W1 dashboard regardless — user.* and organization.id should not appear on public-facing panels.
findings.scope: W1 dashboard scope absorption, or recheck on next session start.
